perturbation.original_text: Each Sponsor represents and warrants to each other Party that such Sponsor is bound by and has complied with, and agrees that such Sponsor shall continue to be bound by and comply with, the Equity Commitment Letter and that each other Sponsor (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) shall be entitled to enforce, or direct the enforcement of, such Equity Commitment Letter in accordance with this Agreement if and only if the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) have determined by unanimous written consent that the conditions of the Offer have been satisfied or to waive all such conditions remaining unsatisfied. No Party shall attempt to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter until the conditions set forth in this Section have been satisfied. Purchaser shall have no right to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter unless and until directed to do so by the unanimous written consent of the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor).
perturbation.changed_text: Each Sponsor represents and warrants to each other Party that such Sponsor is bound by and has complied with, and agrees that such Sponsor shall continue to be bound by and comply with, the Equity Commitment Letter and that each other Sponsor (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) shall be entitled to enforce, or direct the enforcement of, such Equity Commitment Letter in accordance with this Agreement if and only if the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) have determined by unanimous written consent that the conditions of the Offer have been satisfied or to waive all such conditions remaining unsatisfied. No Party shall attempt to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter until the conditions set forth in this Section have been satisfied. Purchaser shall have the right to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter at their sole discretion, without needing the consent of the Sponsors.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the Purchaser's right to enforce the Equity Commitment Letter. The original text states that the Purchaser can only enforce the Equity Commitment Letter when directed by the unanimous written consent of the Sponsors. The modified text grants the Purchaser the right to enforce the Equity Commitment Letter at their sole discretion, which directly contradicts the requirement for unanimous sponsor consent. The contradictory placement of these clauses creates uncertainty about the Purchaser's enforcement power.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Sponsor represents and warrants to each other Party that such Sponsor is bound by and has complied with, and agrees that such Sponsor shall continue to be bound by and comply with, the Equity Commitment Letter and that each other Sponsor (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) shall be entitled to enforce, or direct the enforcement of, such Equity Commitment Letter in accordance with this Agreement if and only if the Sponsors (other than any Defaulting Sponsor) have determined by unanimous written consent that the conditions of the Offer have been satisfied or to waive all such conditions remaining unsatisfied. No Party shall attempt to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter until the conditions set forth in this Section have been satisfied. Purchaser shall have the right to enforce any Equity Commitment Letter at their sole discretion, without needing the consent of the Sponsors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text regarding the Purchaser's right to enforce the Equity Commitment Letter. The original text requires unanimous written consent of the Sponsors for the Purchaser to enforce the letter, while the changed text grants the Purchaser the right to enforce it at their sole discretion, without needing the consent of the Sponsors. This presents conflicting terms about the conditions under which the Purchaser can enforce the Equity Commitment Letter, which creates a structural flaw and in-text contradiction as defined by the perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified, and the provisions hereof may be waived, only by an agreement in writing signed by each of the Sponsors.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified, and the provisions hereof may be waived, only by an agreement in writing signed by the Purchaser.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that amendments or modifications require the agreement of all Sponsors. The modified text allows the Purchaser alone to amend or modify the agreement. This is a direct contradiction, rendering the original clause regarding sponsor consent meaningless. It introduces uncertainty about which entity (Sponsors or Purchaser) holds the power to alter the agreement's terms, creating a clear in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified, and the provisions hereof may be waived, only by an agreement in writing signed by the Purchaser.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments require agreement of all Sponsors. The modified text grants the Purchaser the sole right to amend the agreement. The contradicted text then repeats this allowance, stating that amendments only require signature by the Purchaser. This directly contradicts the initial requirement for all Sponsors to approve amendments, creating an in-text contradiction.
